sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check player position and green blocks after LEFT
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Check player position
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'Y':
        print(f"Player: boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Count green blocks
green_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"Green blocks: {len(green_nodes)}")
for g in green_nodes:
    print(f"  Green: boundary={g['boundary']}")

# Check if any green blocks disappeared
# Compare with previous state (9 green blocks)
# If still 9, then walking through doesn't collect them
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

After clicking all 7 green blocks with MOUSE and moving the player to the right, the board transformed to:
